sourceTitle: How Notion can help your marketing team stay organized and efficient

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of creating a team wiki in Notion for a marketing team?

options.text: It automatically generates marketing copy for campaigns.
options.explanation: Notion wikis store knowledge; they don't generate copy automatically.

options.text: It consolidates scattered information into a single, searchable hub.
options.explanation: A wiki brings all team knowledge together, reducing silos and making it easy to find.

options.text: It replaces the need for a CRM system.
options.explanation: A wiki is for documentation, not for managing customer relationships.

options.text: It ensures every team member has edit access to all pages.
options.explanation: Permissions can be customized; not all members need edit rights.

questions.question: How can page and workspace analytics improve a marketing wiki?

options.text: By showing which pages are most viewed, helping keep popular content up‑to‑date.
options.explanation: Analytics reveal engagement, so you can prioritize updates for frequently accessed pages.

options.text: By automatically deleting pages that haven’t been viewed in the last month.
options.explanation: Analytics provide insight but do not delete content automatically.

options.text: By assigning tasks to team members based on page view counts.
options.explanation: Analytics inform decisions; they don’t auto‑assign tasks.

options.text: By converting the most viewed pages into PDFs for offline use.
options.explanation: Exporting to PDF is a manual action; analytics don’t trigger conversion.

questions.question: Which property lets you link individual tasks to larger initiatives in Notion?

options.text: Select property
options.explanation: Select lets you choose from predefined options but doesn’t create a link between databases.

options.text: Relation property
options.explanation: A relation property creates a bidirectional link between a task and a project record.

options.text: Formula property
options.explanation: Formulas calculate values; they don’t establish database connections.

options.text: Rollup property
options.explanation: Rollups display aggregated data from a relation; they rely on a relation first.

questions.question: Which view would be most useful for visualizing a content calendar by publishing date?

options.text: Board view grouped by status
options.explanation: Board view is great for status columns, not for timeline or date‑based planning.

options.text: Calendar view
options.explanation: Calendar view displays items on a monthly/weekly grid based on a date property.

options.text: List view sorted alphabetically
options.explanation: List view shows rows but doesn’t provide a visual date layout.

options.text: Gallery view with cover images
options.explanation: Gallery view emphasizes visuals; it’s not optimal for date‑driven scheduling.

questions.question: What is a benefit of using database templates and recurring tasks in Notion?

options.text: They prevent any team member from editing a task once created.
options.explanation: Templates and recurring tasks automate creation, not editing restrictions.

options.text: They ensure consistent structure for new items and reduce manual entry for repeatable work.
options.explanation: Templates pre‑fill fields, and recurring tasks automatically generate future instances.

options.text: They automatically assign tasks to the highest‑paid employee.
options.explanation: Assignment logic must be set manually; Notion doesn’t auto‑assign based on salary.

options.text: They lock the database so no new items can be added.
options.explanation: Templates and recurring tasks add items; they don’t lock the database.

questions.question: How can meeting notes be automatically turned into actionable tasks?

options.text: By enabling the "Auto‑Task" toggle in the page settings.
options.explanation: There is no generic "Auto‑Task" toggle; the process uses a button block.

options.text: By adding a button in the meeting notes template that creates items in the tasks database.
options.explanation: A button can be configured to push checklist items to a tasks database, automating conversion.

options.text: By tagging the meeting note with @task and Notion automatically creates a task.
options.explanation: Mentions alert people but don’t generate tasks on their own.

options.text: By linking the meeting notes page to the tasks database via a relation and manually copying items.
options.explanation: While a relation can link pages, the automation requires a button, not manual copying.

questions.question: In what way does Notion AI assist with document creation?

options.text: It writes entire documents from a single keyword without any user input.
options.explanation: AI provides suggestions and outlines, but still requires user direction.

options.text: It can generate outlines, draft text, and suggest edits when invoked with the spacebar.
options.explanation: Pressing space brings up the AI menu to help write, edit, or summarize content.

options.text: It automatically formats documents based on brand colors.
options.explanation: AI assists with language, not visual styling.

options.text: It translates documents into multiple languages instantly.
options.explanation: Translation isn’t a core AI feature highlighted in the guide.

questions.question: Which setting controls who can edit, view, or share a page in a Notion wiki?

options.text: Sidebar organization
options.explanation: Sidebar order affects navigation, not permissions.

options.text: Permission settings
options.explanation: Permissions let you grant or restrict edit, view, and share rights per page.

options.text: Template selection
options.explanation: Templates define structure, not access control.

options.text: Relation properties
options.explanation: Relations link databases; they don’t manage page-level permissions.

questions.question: What is the difference between a backlink and a regular link in Notion?

options.text: A backlink shows up at the top of a page indicating other pages that reference it, while a regular link is just an inline reference.
options.explanation: Backlinks provide reverse navigation; regular links only point forward.

options.text: A backlink automatically updates its URL when the target page is renamed, whereas a regular link does not.
options.explanation: Both types update automatically; the key distinction is visibility of inbound connections.

options.text: A backlink can only be added in the sidebar, while a regular link can be placed anywhere in the content.
options.explanation: Backlinks appear in the page header, not the sidebar.

options.text: A backlink creates a duplicate copy of the linked page, while a regular link just points to it.
options.explanation: Backlinks do not duplicate pages; they simply reference the existing page.

questions.question: How does customizing the Notion sidebar improve a marketing team's workflow?

options.text: It allows the team to hide all pages except the wiki, forcing everyone to use only that resource.
options.explanation: Customizing the sidebar is about organization, not restricting access.

options.text: It lets you arrange pages logically and create teamspaces, making it faster for members to locate relevant information.
options.explanation: A well‑structured sidebar reduces time spent searching for pages.

options.text: It automatically syncs the sidebar with external project management tools.
options.explanation: Sidebar customization is internal to Notion; it doesn’t sync externally.

options.text: It changes the color scheme of the workspace to match brand guidelines.
options.explanation: Color theming is separate from sidebar arrangement.
